title: Roof sheeting anchoring system and method
abstract: A roof sheeting anchoring system is disclosed including a channel assembly, a pair of anchoring bodies, and a foot. The channel assembly includes a plurality of fastener holes. The anchoring bodies include a flat and tapered end connected to truss-anchor hooks. The anchoring bodies are each removably insertable into the channel assembly. The foot is rotatably affixable to both of the bodies at the attachment points. Each foot includes at least one fastener hole. The roof sheeting anchoring system securely attaches a roof structure to a truss to prevent separation between the roof and the truss. The method of using a roof sheeting anchoring system is included.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is new and desired to be protected by Leters Patent is set forth in the appended claims: 1. 1. A method of using a roof sheeting anchoring system comprising the steps of: providing a roof sheeting anchoring system including a channel assembly and an anchor assembly comprising two anchoring bodies and a foot; affixing the channel assembly to the roof sheeting; disposing the anchoring assembly around a truss; and inserting the two anchoring bodies into the channel assembly. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising the step of fastening the anchor assembly to the truss. 3. The method of claim 2 wherein the channel assembly further comprises a rectangular channel with an opening, two long tabs extending inward on two sides of the opening and extending along the channel, and a flap extending from a comer of the channel, along the channel, and coplanar to a side of the channel. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein the two anchoring bodies further comprise two anchoring bodies each comprising an elongate member with a flat and tapered end and another end, a truss-anchor hook connected to the flat and tapered end; and a pair of atachment points located near the other end. 5. The method of claim 4 wherein the foot is affixed to both of the anchoring bodies at the atachment points and the anchoring bodies are each removably insertable into the channel assembly. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein the flat and tapered end includes a twist of about 90 degrees. 7. The method of claim 6 wherein the system comprises ferrous or non-ferrous metal. 8. The method of claim 7 wherein the system includes a galvanized or anodized coating. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein a fastener affixes the foot to the anchoring bodies at the attachment points. 10. The method of claim 9 wherein the channel assembly includes a plurality of nail or screw holes. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein the anchoring bodies include at least one nail or screw hole. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein the foot includes at least one nail or screw hole in addition to the attachment points. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the anchoring bodies are removably engaged with the long tabs. 14. The method of claim 13 wherein the foot is rotatably attached to the anchoring bodies. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the elongate member comprises two opposing truss- anchor hooks. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein the anchoring bodies are each removably inserted into the channel assembly. 17. The method of claim 16 wherein the anchoring bodies are removably engaged with the long tabs along the channel assembly. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the flap traps an edge of a sheet of roofing material. 19. The method of claim 18 cups a bottom portion of a truss. 20. The method of claim 1 wherein the system comprises a plastic or a composite material.
